identity.name: ECOSTRESS mineral tir axis d3032c60

top_classes.name: Sample from DeKalb, New York via the Smithsonian (sampleno. NMNH R18685).
Hunt and Salisbury Collection (from the Clay Mineral SocietyRepository #CCA-1), orign, Flagstaff Hill, El Dorado Co., California.
Sample from Baja California, Mexico via Smithsonian (sampleno. NMNH 112513-2.
W. J. Ehmann #40, from Kyogle, N.S.W., Australia.
Donated by W. J. Ehmann , Sample #7 from Castle Creek,Idaho.

Most of the 13 mm x 10 mm x 6 mm sample was clean, fresh and green. One area was eroded and weathered and probably impure. The crushed sample was hand-picked to avoid impurities. A moderate amount of weathering and alteration can be seen under the microscope. The alteration is brown in color (limonite). Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers.(Chain Silicates) (Pyroxene Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.inosilicate.powder.diopsi2.spectrum.txt

The sample was made up of green chunks of coarsely crystalline material. Microscopic examination indicated that it was pure.Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers.(sheet silicates) (Chlorite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.phyllosilicate.powder.clinoc2.spectrum.txt

The sample was one large (2.5 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm) crystal with good crystal faces, weighing 19 g. It was dark gray in color, with <<1% limonitic surface contamination, which was easily removed. Under the microscope the sample appears to be pure and free from alteration. Sample was in Epidote Group and particle size was less than 2 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.sorosilicate.powder.clinoz1p.spectrum.txt

The sample was a white crystalline mass. Petrographically the sample appears to be pure chabazite. Sample was <2 Micrometers in particle size. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.tectosilicate.powder.chabaz1.spectrum.txt

The sample is a cream-colored earthy mass. Because of the earthy nature of this mineral sample, there is little difference between the spectra of different particle size ranges. Petrographically it is clean and pure clinoptilolite. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers.(Framework silicates) (Zeolite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.tectosilicate.powder.clinop1.spectrum.txt
Meerdink et al. 2019, Baldridge et al. 2009
